title: Java Basics: Print Your First Message

description.task: Write a Java program that prints the message 'I am learning programming with Java!' to the console.
instructions: Inside the main method, use System.out.println() to print the required message.
The message must be exactly: I am learning programming with Java!
description.hint: System.out.println() is the method used to print a line of text in Java. Every Java program starts execution from the main method.
description.expectedOutput: I am learning programming with Java!

description.task: Read the Java code given in the starter and predict what will be printed to the console. Then run the code to verify your prediction.
description.context: Understanding what code will do before running it is a core programming skill called 'code tracing'.
instructions: Before running: write down exactly what you expect to appear in the console.
Note the difference between System.out.println() and System.out.print().
Run the code and confirm your prediction.
In a comment at the top of your submission, write your predicted output.
description.hint: println() adds a newline at the end; print() does NOT. The \n inside a string is an escape sequence that inserts a newline character.

description.task: Write a Java program that prints 5 real-world examples of software programs and the programming language each is primarily built with.
instructions: Print exactly 5 software examples, numbered 1 to 5.
Use the format: '1. Android OS - Java/Kotlin'.
Choose programs from different categories (OS, app, web, game, tool).
Include at least one Java-based program.
description.hint: Examples: Android (Java/Kotlin), Minecraft (Java), Gmail (JavaScript/Java backend), LinkedIn (Java), Twitter backend (Java/Scala).
description.expectedOutput:
1. Android OS - Java/Kotlin
2. Minecraft - Java
3. LinkedIn - Java
4. Gmail (Backend) - Java
5. Eclipse IDE - Java
